executive_summary: The system allows users to register with an email and password, requiring email verification before first login. Registration includes password complexity rules, email format validation, and blocking of disposable email providers. Rate limiting is applied to account creation (5 attempts per IP per 15 minutes) and resend verification requests (3 attempts per account or IP per hour, with a 30-minute lockout). Email verification links expire after 24 hours, and users can request a resend every 60 seconds. Clarifications confirmed password rules, email validation (simplified RFC 5322), error messages for duplicate email, invalid password length, and invalid email format, as well as rate limit details.
functional_summary: The system shall allow a user to create an account using an email address and a password. Immediately after successful registration, the system shall send a unique email verification link that expires in 24 hours. The user may request a resend of the verification email with a 60-second cooldown between requests. The system shall block account creation attempts from a single IP after 5 failed attempts within a 15-minute rolling window. The system shall block resend verification email requests for a specific account or IP after 3 attempts within 1 hour, temporarily disabling this feature for 30 minutes. The system shall prevent a user from logging in for the first time until their email address has been verified.
confirmed_business_rules: Each email address used for registration must be unique across all accounts.
The email verification link sent to the user shall expire 24 hours after it is issued.
The system shall block disposable email providers during registration.
Password must be between 8 and 64 characters.
Password must contain at least one uppercase letter, one lowercase letter, one digit, and one special character (e.g., @, #, $).
Password must not contain personally identifiable information such as the user's name.
Email format must conform to a simplified RFC 5322 standard.
If a user attempts to register with an email that already exists, the system shall display the error message: 'Email này đã được đăng ký. Vui lòng đăng nhập hoặc sử dụng email khác.'
If the password does not meet length or complexity requirements, the system shall display the error message: 'Mật khẩu phải có độ dài từ 8 đến 64 ký tự.'
If the email format is invalid, the system shall display the error message: 'Địa chỉ email không đúng định dạng. Vui lòng kiểm tra lại.'
Account creation rate limit: maximum 5 failed attempts per IP within a 15-minute rolling window.
Resend verification email rate limit: maximum 3 attempts per account or IP within 1 hour, after which the feature is disabled for 30 minutes.
validation_rules: Password length must be between 8 and 64 characters.
Password must contain at least one uppercase letter, one lowercase letter, one digit, and one special character (e.g., @, #, $).
Password must not contain personally identifiable information such as the user's name.
Email format must conform to a simplified RFC 5322 standard.
If a user attempts to register with an email that already exists, the system shall display the error message: 'Email này đã được đăng ký. Vui lòng đăng nhập hoặc sử dụng email khác.'
If the password does not meet length or complexity requirements, the system shall display the error message: 'Mật khẩu phải có độ dài từ 8 đến 64 ký tự.'
If the email format is invalid, the system shall display the error message: 'Địa chỉ email không đúng định dạng. Vui lòng kiểm tra lại.'
open_questions: The specific list or source of disposable email providers to be blocked is not defined.
The exact behavior when the email verification link expires is not specified (e.g., can the user request a new link after expiration, or must they re-register?).
The error message to display when the password contains spaces or personal information is not specified.
The error message to display when the email is from a blocked disposable provider is not specified.
The error message to display when rate limits are exceeded for account creation or resend verification is not specified.
assumptions: The external email delivery service is assumed to be reliable and available for sending verification emails.
The list of disposable email providers will be maintained and updated regularly to prevent outdated blocking.
The simplified RFC 5322 validation is assumed to be a practical regex or parser that covers common valid email formats without full RFC compliance.
risks: The email verification link expiration of 24 hours may be too short for users who do not check their email promptly, leading to a poor user experience or account abandonment.
Rate limiting on account creation (5 attempts per IP per 15 minutes) may inadvertently block legitimate users behind a shared IP (e.g., corporate network or school).
The list of disposable email providers may become outdated, allowing users to register with temporary emails and bypass verification or create fraudulent accounts.
